title: City of Reno Boards and Commissions 2026 Q4 Vacancy Report
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: April 22, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cali Shy, Agenda Coordinator
DEPT: City Manager's Office
SUBJECT: City of Reno Boards and Commissions 2026 Q4 Vacancy Report

This memo is intended to provide an overview of current vacancies on the City of Reno's boards and commissions, as well as vacancies anticipated within the next 90 days as of April 1, 2026. Changes in membership occurring after April 1, 2026 are not reflected.

The scope of this report is limited to internal boards and commissions for which appointments are made directly by the Reno City Council. This report's purpose is to assist the Mayor and City Council in planning for upcoming appointments and reappointments, particularly to the boards for which the Mayor and City Council serve as Council liaisons. To further support this process, staff will also meet individually with each Council Member throughout the quarter to review vacancies and applications for the boards each Council Member recommends appointments to. These meetings will help ensure timely appointments and reappointments as vacancies occur.

fields.tldr: This memo provides the City of Reno's Q4 2026 vacancy report for internal boards and commissions as of April 1, 2026. It covers current vacancies and those anticipated within the next 90 days across 19 boards and commissions. The report is intended to help the Mayor and City Council plan upcoming appointments and reappointments. Staff will also meet individually with each Council Member during the quarter to review vacancies and applications.
fields.key_stats: Current vacancies total approximately 37 open positions across multiple boards, including 6 on the Access Advisory Board, 4 on the Senior Residents Advisory Board, 4 on the Urban Forestry Commission, 4 on the Ward 1 NAB, 3 on the Ward 2 NAB, 6 on the Ward 4 NAB, 1 on the Ward 5 NAB, 6 on the Ward 6 NAB, 2 on the Ward 3 NAB, and 1 on the Reno City Charter Committee. Upcoming vacancies within 90 days include 10 named individuals across 6 boards with terms ending April 30 or May 31, 2026.
fields.key_decisions: The Mayor and City Council need to fill current vacancies and plan reappointments or new appointments for 10 upcoming term expirations across the Historical Resources Commission, Civil Service Commission, Building Enterprise Fund Advisory Committee, Arts and Culture Advisory Board, and Ward 2 and Ward 4 Neighborhood Advisory Boards.
fields.action_items: Council Members should meet individually with staff during Q4 to review vacancies and applications for boards they liaison; appointments and reappointments should be made for all current and upcoming vacancies, particularly those expiring April 30 and May 31, 2026.
